In an earlier interview with [Boerenbusiness](https://www.boerenbusiness.nl/artikelen/10914505/kijk-door-ogen-van-ander-naar-je-eigen-informatie) jury chairman Laurens Sloot encouraged companies in the agri and food sector to participate.

metadata.source_title: Final call for Agribusiness Award - innovations deserve a podium

text: Laurens Sloot. Jury chairman Agribusiness Awards, Director and professor of Retail & Entrepreneurship. EFMI Business School and University of Groningen. In an earlier interview with [Boerenbusiness](https://www.boerenbusiness.nl/artikelen/10914505/kijk-door-ogen-van-ander-naar-je-eigen-informatie) jury chairman Laurens Sloot encouraged companies in the agri and food sector to participate. "It broadens your network and a strong network promotes innovation." The director of EFMI Business School and part-time professor of Retail & Entrepreneurship at the University of Groningen joined the jury of the [Agribusiness Award](http://agribusinessaward.nl/) last year.

metadata.source_title: Final call for Agribusiness Award - innovations deserve a podium

text: Companies that want to register their innovation for the Agribusiness Award 2026 must take a number of [conditions] into account (http://agribusinessaward.nl/regulations). As jury chairman, Sloot mainly looks at how innovative and distinctive the innovation is. But also whether it is mainly an idea or a concept or whether the innovation has already proven itself. "And we look at whether there is a potentially strong revenue model. Something will only fly if companies can actually earn something from it." The reason for Sloot to contribute to the Agribusiness Award is the impact that the election makes. > “With the great visibility of the Agribusiness Award, it is not only a compliment to the winning company, but also a calling card for the sector as a whole.”.
